layoutRegions.name: masthead
layoutRegions.purpose: Secret Service app identity, current inspection posture, and the primary staged cleanup action.

layoutRegions.name: provider-grid
layoutRegions.purpose: Provider, unlock-state, secret-count, and stale-entry scan rows that stay keyboard reachable.

layoutRegions.name: audit-rail
layoutRegions.purpose: Application access grants, rollback-safe cleanup steps, and command probes for credential review.

components.name: ProviderRow
components.purpose: Focusable Secret Service provider row with unlock status, stored secret count, and stale secret count.
interactions: tab order follows the provider scan order
hover and focus use raised surface
focus ring remains visible inside dense rows

components.name: StateBadge
components.purpose: Semantic ready, review, and locked labels for providers, grants, cleanup steps, and probes.

components.name: ProbeCard
components.purpose: Focusable command item for Secret Service inspection commands and rollback confirmation steps.
interactions: command text wraps without changing grid columns
tab order reaches every probe before leaving the inspector

acceptanceChecks: Generated app imports src/omarchy-theme.css before app styles.
Every visible color comes from a --omarchy-* CSS variable or color-mix using one.
Every button, row, input, and command item has a visible :focus-visible state.
Layout remains stable at 320px, 768px, and 1280px viewport widths.
Primary content is usable on first load without a marketing interstitial.
A .desktop launcher can be generated with omarchy-native app desktop.
An opt-in theme sync hook can be generated with omarchy-native app hook.
